zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Elizabeth Agnes Osborne於1480年出生在威爾士布洛可爾時，她的父親Sir Hugo Osborne25歲，母親Lady Annie Legate30歲。她於1502年與Sir Richard Day "Baron of Worfield"在英國史洛普爾紐波特結婚。他們至少是14個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1580年11月4日在英國倫敦享年100歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭米德萊可斯特普尼聖 登斯坦 安德 哥吉拉 沙智 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Elizabeth Agnes Osborne于1480年出生在威尔士布洛可爾时，她的父亲Sir Hugo Osborne25岁，母亲Lady Annie Legate30岁。她于1502年与Sir Richard Day "Baron of Worfield"在英国史洛普爾紐波特结婚。他们至少是14个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1580年11月4日在英国倫敦享年100岁，埋葬在英国英格兰米德萊可斯特普尼聖 登斯坦 安德 哥吉拉 沙智 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Osborne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Osbern, partly from late Old English Ōsbern, an Anglicized form of Old Norse Ásbiǫrn (from áss ‘god’ + biǫrn ‘bear’), but more usually from Norman use of the cognate ancient Germanic Ōsbern. Osbern was often treated as an alternative form of Osbert. For pet forms see Hoskin and Opie . The surname has also been established in Ireland (Waterford, Tipperary) since the mid 16th century.

variants.name: Oborne
meanings: habitational name from Oborne in Dorset. The placename derives from Old English wōh ‘twisted, crooked’ + burna ‘spring, stream’.
possibly from the Middle English personal name Obern, an Anglo-Norman French form of Osbern (see Osborne ).

variants.name: Hoskin
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Osekin with prosthetic H-, a pet form of Anglo-Norman French names beginning with Os- (particularly Osbert, as well as Osbern, and Osmund) + the Flemish diminutive suffix -kin. All three personal names were current in medieval Cornwall or Devon; see Osborne , Osmond , and Opie (a pet form of Osbert). Old English Ōsbeorn, Ōsbeohrt, and Ōsmund may also have contributed in a minor way to Middle English usage; Middle English Oswald is another possible source, but the name was very rare in the late 14th century. For the excrescent initial H-, compare Herrick .

variants.name: Oborn
meanings: English (Somerset): habitational name chiefly from Oborne in Dorset, or perhaps occasionally from Howbourne Farm in Hadlow Down, Sussex, Woburn in Bedfordshire, Woburn Park in Surrey, or Wooburn in Buckinghamshire. The placenames derive from Old English wōh ‘twisted, crooked’ + burna ‘spring, stream’, except Wooburn in Buckinghamshire, which is of uncertain etymology.
English (of Norman origin): perhaps from the Middle English personal name Obern, an Anglo-Norman French form of Osbern (see Osborne ).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Elizabeth Agnes Osborne was born in 1480, in Pembrokeshire, Wales, her father, Sir Hugo Osborne, was 25 and her mother, Lady Annie Legate, was 30. She married Sir Richard Day "Baron of Worfield" in 1502, in Newport, Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 14 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 4 November 1580, in London, England, at the age of 100, and was buried in Saint Dunstan and All Saints Church, Stepney, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Elizabeth Agnes Osborne est née en 1480, à Pembrokeshire, Galles, son père, Sir Hugo Osborne, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Lady Annie Legate, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Richard Day "Baron of Worfield" en 1502, à Newport, Shropshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 14 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 4 novembre 1580, à Londres, Angleterre, à l’âge de 100 ans, et a été enterrée à Saint Dunstan and All Saints Church, Stepney, Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Elizabeth Agnes Osborne è nata il nel 1480 nel Pembrokeshire, Galles, sua padre, Sir Hugo Osborne, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Lady Annie Legate, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Sir Richard Day "Baron of Worfield" nel 1502, nel Newport, Shropshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 14 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 4 novembre 1580, nel London, England, all'età di 100 anni, e fu sepolta a Saint Dunstan and All Saints Church, Stepney, Middlesex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Agnes Osborneが1480年にウェールズペンブロケシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir Hugo Osborneは25歳，母Lady Annie Legateは30歳であった。1502年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアニュープオルトでSir Richard Day "Baron of Worfield"と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも14人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1580年11月4日にイギリスロンドンで100歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスミッドレセックスステプニーサイント ダンスタン アンド アル サインツ チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.Saint Dunstan and All Saints Church, Ste: 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 스테프니 세인트 던스탠 앤드 앨 세인츠 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Agnes Osborne(이)가 1480년 웨일스 p브로케실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Hugo Osborne(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Annie Legate(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1502년 영국 슈럽실르 뉴포트에서 Sir Richard Day "Baron of Worfield"(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1580년 11월 4일 영국 런던에서 100세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 스테프니 세인트 던스탠 앤드 앨 세인츠 처르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Elizabeth Agnes Osborne nasceu em 1480, em Pembrokeshire, Gales, seu pai, Sir Hugo Osborne, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Lady Annie Legate, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Richard Day "Baron of Worfield" em 1502, em Newport, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 14 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 4 de novembro de 1580, em London, Inglaterra, com 100 anos, e foi sepultada em Saint Dunstan and All Saints Church, Stepney, Middlesex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Saint Dunstan and All Saints Church, Ste: сеинт дунстань анд алл сеинц чурч, степни, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Elizabeth Agnes Osborne родилась в 1480 г. в местности пемброкешир, Уэльс, её отцу по имени Sir Hugo Osborne было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Annie Legate, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Richard Day "Baron of Worfield" в 1502 г. в местности нюпорт, шропшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 14 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 4 ноября 1580 г. в местности Лондон, Англия в возрасте 100 лет и была похоронена в местности сеинт дунстань анд алл сеинц чурч, степни, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Elizabeth Agnes Osborne nació en 1480, en Pembrokeshire, Gales, su papá, Sir Hugo Osborne, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Lady Annie Legate, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Sir Richard Day "Baron of Worfield" en 1502, en Newport, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 14 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 4 de noviembre de 1580, en London, Inglaterra, a la edad de 100 años, y fue sepultada en Saint Dunstan and All Saints Church, Stepney, Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
